title: Client.txt zone-change watcher (logwatch.py)
context: PoECraft v1. Implements poecraft/logwatch.py: tails the PoE Client.txt log and fires a refresh callback on zone-change lines. Refresh is zone-change-driven ONLY (settled in grill — no polling timer in v1). Verified path on this machine: ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Path of Exile/logs/Client.txt (the Proton-prefix Documents/My Games/Path of Exile location is empty). Zone-change line confirmed against the real log: '[INFO Client <pid>] : You have entered <Area Name>.' Adds a Config field client_log_path (default '' => auto-discover the Steam install logs path). Core parsing/tailing is synchronous + unit-testable with fixture files; the async loop is a thin wrapper. Depends on recipe-core scaffolding (pytest, tests/, jj repo).
architecture: poecraft/logwatch.py: parse_zone_change(line: str) -> str | None — area name for lines matching the You-have-entered pattern, else None. default_log_path() -> Path | None — checks common Steam roots (~/.local/share/Steam, ~/.steam/steam) for steamapps/common/Path of Exile/logs/Client.txt. resolve_log_path(config) -> Path | None — config.client_log_path if set, else default_log_path(). ClientLogWatcher(path, on_zone_change: Callable[[str], Awaitable[None]], poll_interval=0.5): holds last_size; _process_new(text) -> list[str] (pure, testable); _poll() reads appended bytes since last_size, dispatches callback per zone change, guards truncation; async start()/stop(). Config change in poecraft/config.py: add client_log_path: str = '' field.

red.description: In tests/test_logwatch.py, parse_zone_change on a real line '... [INFO Client 340] : You have entered Divided Hideout.' == 'Divided Hideout'. Fails (module missing).

green.description: Implement poecraft/logwatch.py: parse_zone_change(line)->str|None using regex for the You-have-entered pattern. Pass.

red.description: Chat lines, other INFO lines (e.g. 'Connected to ams.login...'), and blank lines -> None. Fails.
With a fake Steam root containing common/Path of Exile/logs/Client.txt, default_log_path() returns it; resolve_log_path(config_with_explicit_path) returns the explicit path. Fails.

green.description: Implement default_log_path (checks common Steam roots) + resolve_log_path; make the Steam roots list injectable for tests. Pass.
Add client_log_path: str = '' to Config in poecraft/config.py; update config.example.yaml with the field + comment pointing at the verified Steam path; load_config validates the example.

steps.name: ClientLogWatcher._process_new — extracts zone changes from appended text

red.description: _process_new on a block with one zone-change line among noise returns ['Lioneye area']; a block with no zone change returns []. Fails.

steps.name: ClientLogWatcher — appends-only tail over a fixture file

red.description: Write a fixture Client.txt; create a watcher with a recording fake callback; call internal _poll() once (no new data) -> no callback; append a zone-change line; _poll() -> callback called once with the area name. Fails.

green.description: Implement size-based tailing + callback dispatch in _poll. Pass.

red.description: Rewrite the fixture file smaller; _poll() resets offset without error and fires no spurious callback. Fails.

green.description: Implement async start()/stop(); exercise via an integration test using asyncio + a fixture file + a zone-change append and a tiny poll interval; assert callback fired, then stop() cleanly. (Add pytest-asyncio as a dev dep if needed.)

design.currentState: No logwatch.py. Config has no client_log_path field. Client.txt verified at the Steam install logs dir. recipe-core scaffolding (pytest, tests/, jj) exists.
design.desiredState: logwatch.py with parse_zone_change, default_log_path, resolve_log_path, ClientLogWatcher (sync _process_new/_poll tested; async start/stop). Config.client_log_path added. Suite green.
design.patterns: TDD. Core parsing/tailing synchronous and unit-tested with fixture files; async loop a thin wrapper. Injectable Steam roots for tests. Fake recording callback.
design.decisions: Zone-change-driven refresh only (no polling timer). Zone line matched via the You-have-entered pattern. Default path auto-discovered from Steam install logs dir; explicit config wins. Truncation resets offset.
design.questions: Should start/stop async lifecycle be tested with pytest-asyncio or kept structural? (lean: structural; test the sync path thoroughly)
